json.engineerPerspective: iOS/web developer brokering between Railway deployment state, Xcode builds, browser/iOS console logs, Linear issue tracking, and the agent — constantly relaying runtime observations and deployment status the agent cannot see.

fixableInteractions.title: Hooks blocking xcodebuild — human becomes the build button
fixableInteractions.interactionSurface: Xcode build system <-> Agent (blocked by hooks)
fixableInteractions.humanRole: Human manually presses Cmd+R in Xcode because agent's xcodebuild commands are blocked by PreToolUse hooks
fixableInteractions.description: The agent explicitly asked the human to 'Hit Cmd+R in Xcode' because hooks were blocking xcodebuild commands. The human literally becomes the build trigger.

fixableInteractions.integration: Add an exclusion pattern in the PreToolUse:Bash hook for xcodebuild and xcrun commands, or create a dedicated iOS build hook that allows these through
fixableInteractions.observableSuccess: Agent runs xcodebuild directly without human pressing Cmd+R
fixableInteractions.whyNotJustJudgment: Building code is mechanical — the human is not making a design choice, they're pressing a button

fixableInteractions.humanRole: Human tails daemon logs, reads Railway dashboard, opens browser DevTools, and pastes relevant log lines into Claude Code

fixableInteractions.integration: Agent should run `tail -n 50 ~/.implicit/daemon.log` after daemon changes. Use `railway logs` for deployment logs. Use Playwright console_messages for browser logs. Add a post-deployment verification skill.

json.engineerPerspective: Engineer building AgentScout itself — meta-tool for diagnosing Claude Code usage. Human orchestrates the CLI pipeline (emit-prompts → subagents → apply-answers), relays external context from stakeholders and X/Twitter, and shepherds stop hook checkpoints.

fixableInteractions.title: Human manually pipes CLI stages (emit-prompts → apply-answers)
fixableInteractions.humanRole: Runs CLI commands, cats output files, pipes between stages
fixableInteractions.description: Every /diagnose run requires manual: (1) node dist/cli.js --emit-prompts, (2) cat output files, (3) feed to subagents, (4) cat subagent outputs, (5) pipe to --apply-answers. The human is the shell script.
fixableInteractions.existingToolCheck: The /diagnose command IS the orchestrator — but it delegates plumbing to the human instead of chaining steps internally.
fixableInteractions.integration: The /diagnose command should chain these steps: emit-prompts → split briefs → spawn subagents → collect results → apply-answers. The human should only see the final output.
fixableInteractions.observableSuccess: Human runs /diagnose once and gets results without manual cat/pipe commands
fixableInteractions.whyNotJustJudgment: Pipeline plumbing is deterministic shell work
evidence: AGENTSCOUT_LLM_PROJECT_LIMIT=10 node dist/cli.js --emit-prompts

json.engineerPerspective: Building a landing page. Sessions devolved into a prolonged font-matching ordeal where the human acted as the visual comparator between localhost and implicit.cc, repeatedly correcting the agent's font identification. 75% of interventions were the human being the diff engine between two browser tabs.

fixableInteractions.title: Agent cannot visually compare two live sites
fixableInteractions.humanRole: Switches between two browser tabs comparing fonts, identifies mismatches, tells agent which element has which font
fixableInteractions.description: 12 of 16 interventions were the human acting as a visual comparison bridge. The agent has Playwright but never used it to screenshot both URLs and compare computed styles. User eventually told the agent to use Playwright explicitly.
fixableInteractions.existingToolCheck: Playwright plugin available. gstack-browser-use skill available. Neither was used until the user demanded it. Even then, the agent failed to correctly identify the target element.
fixableInteractions.integration: CLAUDE.md rule: 'When matching visual styles from a reference URL, use Playwright to: (1) screenshot both URLs, (2) extract computed styles from target elements via browser_evaluate, (3) compare and apply. Never guess fonts — always verify programmatically.'
fixableInteractions.observableSuccess: Agent screenshots both sites and extracts computed styles without human tab-switching
fixableInteractions.whyNotJustJudgment: Font IDENTIFICATION is a verifiable fact. Font CHOICE is judgment. The agent was failing at identification.
